QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $719.79 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source â€” may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Stocks hit new records Thursday as the Dow reclaimed 50,000 and semis led the AI rally, but Asian shares dived overnight after the hottest CPI since 2023 (3.8% headline) and Fed-hawk chatter revived rate-hike anxiety. The Trump-Xi summit produced a pledge to keep Hormuz open and talks of tariff cuts on $30B of goods, but no trade deal was signed. Meanwhile, WTI held above $103 as Iran seized another vessel, and Kevin Warsh takes the Fed chair today with Powell's last day.
commodities_note: WTI rose 1% to $103.39 on fresh Hormuz ship seizures and an Iranian drone-damaged tanker leaking fuel, despite 30 vessels passing the strait. Gold eased 0.9% to $4,582.40 as real yields ticked higher, while the US10Y slipped slightly to 4.46%. The QQQ price ($719.79) flagged a data anomaly â€” potential unit mismatch or genuine move; the index rose 0.21% on the day and the SOXX benchmark slipped 0.28% to $530.03.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain hard-blocked on overbought RSI and extension. DBS and OCBC are >6% above their 50-day, UOB at 2.8% but RSI 65 triggers the mechanical block. The dual NIM squeeze from US10Y above 4.20% and strong SGD persists with no relief in sight until the MAS October decision. No divergence within the cluster â€” all are in the same tight-extension posture.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by NIM compression â€” await rate relief
singapore.key_development: No relief day 56; US10Y at 4.46% remains above the 4.20% reset threshold, and MAS reaffirmed S$NEER pace in April 2026.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX slipped 0.28% but remains elevated near $530 after a 31.9% monthly surge. The cluster is uniformly extended â€” NVDA, AMD, INTC, MU, TSEM, 000660.KS, and LITE are all beyond the 20% extreme-extension block. ASML, TSM, and AVGO sit in the 12-20% band, all hard-blocked by RSI or insufficient trend duration. No single name offers a working entry; the AI capex supercycle is priced in and needs consolidation before fresh money can be deployed.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish (AI demand), tactically extended â€” no entries available
semiconductors.key_development: Cisco's AI networking supercycle and TSEM's $1.3B SiPho deals reinforced demand, but the entire cluster is too stretched for entry.

big_tech.summary: MSFT is the only BigTech name near a workable entry, just 2.7% above its 50-day with RSI 50.3 and a 1.5:1 R:R. AMZN and GOOG are hard-blocked above 10% extension, while PLTR remains CAUTION on valuation despite a potential 2.1:1 setup. The cluster's divergence is MSFT vs. the extended pair; MSFT's proximity to the 50-day makes it the highest-conviction name in the watchlist for near-term action.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed â€” MSFT near entry, AMZN/GOOG extended, PLTR valuation risk
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft's LinkedIn layoffs and Xbox multiplayer paywall removal are incremental; the real watch is Azure non-OpenAI RPO trajectory.

active_risks: Iran Hormuz blockade: fresh vessel seizures and drone attacks escalate oil supply disruption â€” WTI above $103, ship traffic far below normal.
US-China summit concludes without a trade deal, but Trump-Xi agreed to keep Hormuz open and discussed $30B tariff cuts via a Board of Trade.
Fed hawkish pivot risk: April CPI 3.8% and Collins hint at possible rate hikes; new chair Kevin Warsh faces immediate inflation test.
US semiconductor export controls: H200 clearance to Chinese firms announced but no deliveries yet, while broader tech decoupling persists.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's rise directly hits transport and consumer stocks, while semis rally on Trump-Xi easing hopes and AI capex. Energy premiums lift defense (PLTR) but compress BigTech margins. Singapore banks remain squeezed by elevated US rates and strong SGD.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump-Xi summit ended with no trade deal, but a pledge to keep Hormuz open. US Trade Rep Greer outlined $30B tariff-cut mechanism and a Board of Trade. Warsh's first day as Fed chair could bring hawkish policy signal.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries while VIX is low but extension blocks most names and macro risks remain elevated.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Twenty of 22 names are in CAUTION, with 14 of those hard-blocked on extreme extension (>20% above 50-day) and no actionable entry in the entire semis cluster.
